structures.name: Holly Lisle's Clinic Method
structures.description: A revision-first workflow for diagnosing a draft: define the core story question, audit scenes for purpose, then rebuild conflict, character, and language until the manuscript earns its ending.
structures.author: Holly Lisle’s clinic-style revision process for novelists.

phases.title: Core Story Question
phases.description: Name the single question the narrative must answer so every scene can be judged against it.

phases.title: Conflict and Stakes Audit
phases.description: Find where conflict softens, repeats, or goes missing—and what is truly at risk if the hero fails.

phases.title: Character Arc Diagnosis
phases.description: Check want vs. need, lie vs. truth, and whether behavior change tracks the plot.

phases.title: Scene-Level Problem Spots
phases.description: Flag scenes that confuse, stall, contradict earlier setup, or dodge the core conflict.

phases.title: Structural Repair Plan
phases.description: Prioritize fixes: plot logic, character motivation, pacing, then line-level polish.

phases.title: Rewrite Pass
phases.description: Apply targeted rewrites scene by scene, keeping the repair plan in view.

phases.title: Verification Pass
phases.description: Cold read for clarity, emotional payoff, and whether the core question is fully answered.
